# Gal Goldman

> Personal site and technical publication by Gal Goldman, a senior software engineer and architect focused on distributed systems, cloud architecture, production AI systems, engineering quality, interview preparation, and long-term technical learning.

## Main Content

- [Home](https://gel.github.io/): Overview of Gal Goldman, current focus areas, and the main sections of the site.
- [Resume](https://gel.github.io/resume/): Career history across AWS, Amazon, and Intel, with experience in distributed systems, cloud architecture, and AI platforms.
- [Blog](https://gel.github.io/blog/): Technical writing on distributed systems, platform architecture, cloud optimization, and production AI engineering.
- [Projects](https://gel.github.io/projects/): Selected public projects across interactive interview practice, technical publishing, GenAI education, and applied computer vision.
- [Generative AI](https://gel.github.io/generative-ai/): Structured guide to generative AI foundations, research, implementation patterns, and production tradeoffs.
- [Interviews](https://gel.github.io/interviews/): Interview preparation material covering career moves, data structures, system design, and live coding practice.

## Recent Writing

- [Data Platform Myths: What Breaks at Scale and How to De-risk It](https://gel.github.io/blog/data-platform-myths/): Framework for deciding when to build shared platforms versus domain-owned solutions.
- [Profit in the Stack: Why We Need Low-Level Optimization](https://gel.github.io/blog/profit-in-the-stack/): Why hardware-aware optimization matters as software margins tighten.
- [Building Trustworthy AI Agents by Abstracting Infrastructure](https://gel.github.io/blog/agentcore-observability/): Notes on infrastructure abstraction and observability for AI agents.

## Optional

- [Books](https://gel.github.io/books/): Reading notes, ratings, and curated book lists across multiple domains.
- [Podcasts](https://gel.github.io/podcasts/): Podcast notes and selected talks on AI, programming, systems, and product thinking.
